articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for mobile devices that enables offline function calling for tasks like calendar management and device control. This innovation enhances user experience by providing fast, efficient AI capabilities directly on devices.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes mobile AI by enabling complex tasks offline, enhancing user convenience.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
articles.snippet: Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports a robust ecosystem of third-party tools and integrations. Connect your agents to GitHub, Notion, Hugging Face, and more to build capable, real-world applications.
articles.ai_summary: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features integrations with third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, allowing developers to create more capable AI agents. This ecosystem enhances the functionality and versatility of AI applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers to build more robust AI applications through diverse integrations.

articles.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
articles.snippet: Google I/O 2026 is returning May 19-20 at Shoreline Amphitheatre in Mountain View, CA. But before the keynotes begin, you can get into the spirit of the event with our annual tradition: the save the date puzzle.  This year's experience highlights how AI can empower and accelerate

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring keynotes and specialized tracks on AI advancements. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration for developers.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the value of in-person events for networking and skill development in AI.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering improved GPU acceleration and support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance the efficiency of deploying generative AI and on-device inference.
articles.why_it_matters: Positions LiteRT as a key framework for advanced AI deployment, boosting performance.

articles.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
articles.snippet: Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly, interactive "technical gym session" led by Google Cloud experts to help developers and architects sharpen their cloud skills. Moving beyond passive slide decks, the program focuses on hands-on building, covering advanced topics like AI agents, Vertex AI, and developer productivity tools. Each hour-long session is designed to provide tangible results that participants can immediately deploy into their own workflows. It serves as a consistent, dedicated space for
articles.ai_summary: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud experts designed to help developers improve their cloud skills through hands-on activities. This initiative aims to provide practical knowledge and immediate application in workflows.
articles.why_it_matters: Encourages active learning and skill enhancement in cloud development.

articles.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
articles.snippet: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced a suite of updates focused on streamlining the core coding workflow through high-velocity tools like Agent Mode with Auto Approve and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements, along with new features for precise context management and custom commands, aim to transform the AI from a general assistant into a highly tailored, seamless collaborator that adapts to your specific development style.
articles.ai_summary: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to enhance coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more tailored and effective collaborator for developers.
articles.why_it_matters: Transforms AI into a more personalized coding assistant, improving developer productivity.

articles.ai_summary: An analysis maps out the various products Microsoft has named 'Copilot,' showcasing the company's extensive integration of AI across its offerings. This reflects Microsoft's commitment to AI innovation.

articles.title: GPT-5.4 Pro jumps to 150 IQ on MESNA Norway test as OpenAI breaks its own record
articles.snippet: OpenAI’s latest GPT-5.4 Pro model has now achieved an IQ score higher than 99.96% of all human beings, giving markets a fresh signal that AI capability gains are starting to outpace the usual product-cycle noise. OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 Pro touches 150 on public IQ …
articles.ai_summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Pro model has achieved an IQ score of 150, surpassing 99.96% of humans, indicating significant advancements in AI capabilities. This milestone suggests that AI development is accelerating beyond traditional expectations.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals a new era of AI capabilities that could reshape industries.

articles.title: How to opt out of AI data collection in popular apps
articles.snippet: Most AI platforms use your conversations to train their systems by default. Learn how to turn off data collection on ChatGPT, Google, Alexa and Siri.
articles.ai_summary: Users can now learn how to opt out of AI data collection in popular apps like ChatGPT, Google, Alexa, and Siri, enhancing privacy control. This move addresses growing concerns over data usage and user consent.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers users to take control of their data privacy in AI applications.

articles.title: idliappam added to PyPI
articles.snippet: Python SDK for AI Commerce OS — WhatsApp, invoicing, inventory, loyalty, support
articles.ai_summary: The Python SDK for AI Commerce OS, named idliappam, has been added to PyPI, facilitating integration with WhatsApp, invoicing, inventory, and support. This addition enhances the tools available for developers in AI commerce.
articles.why_it_matters: Expands developer resources for AI-driven commerce solutions.

articles.title: Apple’s latest acquisition is telling sign of where the company is headed
articles.snippet: Last month, Apple acquired a popular Final Cut Pro plugin maker: MotionVFX. It isn’t clear what this acquisition will turn into, but based on the fact that it quickly followed the launch of Apple Creator Studio, it’s highly plausible that Apple will integrate…
articles.ai_summary: Apple's acquisition of MotionVFX, a Final Cut Pro plugin maker, hints at future integrations with Apple Creator Studio. This acquisition reflects Apple's strategy to enhance its creative software offerings.
articles.why_it_matters: Indicates Apple's commitment to expanding its creative software ecosystem.

articles.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage

articles.title: AI Is Killing Microsoft
articles.snippet: Microsoft's AI assistant, Copilot, is struggling to show traction, causing the tech giant to continue falling behind. Investors are not happy.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's AI assistant, Copilot, is struggling to gain traction, leading to dissatisfaction among investors and concerns about the company's competitive position. This situation reflects the challenges Microsoft faces in the AI space.
articles.why_it_matters: Reveals the competitive pressures Microsoft faces in the evolving AI landscape.
